title: Former Syracuse vocational school could be demolished for 138-unit apartment building
summary: City officials are negotiating with Philadelphia-based mixed-income developer Pennrose and local developer Luke Esposito to demolish the city-owned Johnson Vocational Center and construct a 138-unit apartment building with ground-floor commercial space. The project is part of a larger downtown revitalization corridor near the I-81 viaduct removal, expected to add 600+ apartments to the area and advance the city's affordable housing and economic development objectives.

entity_refs.name: Michael Collins
entity_refs.role: Commissioner of Neighborhood and Business Development

political_signal.description: Project alignment with stated city priorities: affordable housing integration (mixed-income requirement), downtown economic revitalization, job creation via commercial space, and I-81 corridor transformation. Developer selection emphasizes track record in mixed-income development.

processing_notes: Article references three concurrent housing projects in I-81 corridor (283, ~300, and 52 units respectively) suggesting coordinated development strategy.
Dollar amounts: $2.25B (state I-81 project), $4.2M (property market value assessment)
Key decision point: Common Council approval pending for appraiser hiring and final negotiated terms
Micron semiconductor complex mentioned as driver of developer interest—potential connection to workforce/economic strategy
School district context: vocational programs relocated to high schools; property no longer needed for original use
